title: Politics Here Are The Top 10 Lies Of Liz Cheney And The January 6th Committee By: Tristan Justice January 06, 2025 8 min
raw_response: {"rating": 6.0, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "Opinion/editorial arguing that Liz Cheney and the Jan. 6 Committee propagated “lies,” with consistent praise for Republican critiques and defense of Donald Trump. The author’s own prose uses pejorative labels, frames the committee as illegitimate and deceptive, and downplays the severity of Jan. 6 — all aligning with Right narratives. Result: Right (6.0). Detailed Analysis: (1) Pejorative, delegitimizing language aimed at Cheney and the committee in the author’s voice (e.g., calling Cheney disgraced, the probe a Soviet-style inquisition, a textbook case of projection; describing Hutchinson’s claims as hysterical and a tale) signals strong one-sided derision; (2) The committee is framed as dishonest and unlawful: the author asserts Pelosi violated House rules, that investigators concealed truths, covered up transcripts, misled witnesses, and targeted private citizens — without offering countervailing perspectives; (3) The author rejects “insurrection” and “democracy almost died” framings as hyperbolic, asserts continuity of government was never jeopardized, and characterizes “insurrection” as a partisan label — a classic Right-leaning minimization of Jan. 6; (4) Trump is defended throughout: the author claims an honest transcript review exonerates him, contends the breach began before he finished speaking, and dismisses claims of his enthusiasm for violence, while undermining opposing testimony via personal attacks; (5) Selective sourcing and emphasis: elevates a House GOP oversight review, Tucker Carlson’s footage, and Sen. Mike Lee’s criticisms, while omitting substantive rebuttals from committee members or independent investigators; (6) Loaded phrasing imputes partisan motive and deceit to Democrats (e.g., conspiracy peddled, exploited scenes, covered up Trump’s plea, quietly corrected), encouraging distrust of Democratic institutions; (7) Structural framing: a top-10 “lies” list and section headers that pre-judge each topic cue a prosecutorial stance rather than balanced analysis, reinforcing a consistent Right-leaning frame."}

parsed_output.explanation: Opinion/editorial arguing that Liz Cheney and the Jan. 6 Committee propagated “lies,” with consistent praise for Republican critiques and defense of Donald Trump. The author’s own prose uses pejorative labels, frames the committee as illegitimate and deceptive, and downplays the severity of Jan. 6 — all aligning with Right narratives. Result: Right (6.0). Detailed Analysis: (1) Pejorative, delegitimizing language aimed at Cheney and the committee in the author’s voice (e.g., calling Cheney disgraced, the probe a Soviet-style inquisition, a textbook case of projection; describing Hutchinson’s claims as hysterical and a tale) signals strong one-sided derision; (2) The committee is framed as dishonest and unlawful: the author asserts Pelosi violated House rules, that investigators concealed truths, covered up transcripts, misled witnesses, and targeted private citizens — without offering countervailing perspectives; (3) The author rejects “insurrection” and “democracy almost died” framings as hyperbolic, asserts continuity of government was never jeopardized, and characterizes “insurrection” as a partisan label — a classic Right-leaning minimization of Jan. 6; (4) Trump is defended throughout: the author claims an honest transcript review exonerates him, contends the breach began before he finished speaking, and dismisses claims of his enthusiasm for violence, while undermining opposing testimony via personal attacks; (5) Selective sourcing and emphasis: elevates a House GOP oversight review, Tucker Carlson’s footage, and Sen. Mike Lee’s criticisms, while omitting substantive rebuttals from committee members or independent investigators; (6) Loaded phrasing imputes partisan motive and deceit to Democrats (e.g., conspiracy peddled, exploited scenes, covered up Trump’s plea, quietly corrected), encouraging distrust of Democratic institutions; (7) Structural framing: a top-10 “lies” list and section headers that pre-judge each topic cue a prosecutorial stance rather than balanced analysis, reinforcing a consistent Right-leaning frame.
